Key findings
6 insights · 20 topics analyzed
01
35% of PR placements generate AI citations
6 of 17 URLs are actively appearing in AI-generated answers across 6 topics — all 6 are NerdWallet pages. Both CNBC URLs show zero citations.
02
Chime's best topic coverage is 8.29% — in Checking Account
In Checking Account — Chime's strongest topic — AI tools still cite a competitor or other source more than 9 out of 10 times. Most other topics sit well below 3%, meaning there's significant room to grow third party-driven visibility across the board.
03
Early Paycheck & Build Credit are the biggest uncovered topics
Combined, these two topics generate 215K citations in 30 days with zero PR placement. Early Paycheck (109K) and Build Credit (106K) dwarf every covered topic in raw volume.

Gap opportunities
High-value placements not yet secured
Priority 01 — Largest volume gap
Early Paycheck
109K
Topic citations (30d)
0
PR placements
0%
PR share
wellsfargo.com/checking/early-pay-day/ — 2,049 citations (1.9%)
bankrate.com/banking/checking/best-banks-early-direct-deposit/ — 1,821 citations (1.7%)
Wells Fargo and Bankrate lead every AI answer on early direct deposit — neither mentions Chime. This is Chime's most differentiated product feature and its biggest citation gap. The highest-priority PR placement in this account.
Priority 02 — New gap identified
Build Credit
105K
Topic citations (30d)
0
PR placements
0%
PR share
discover.com/credit-cards/secured-credit-card/ — 1,291 citations (1.2%)
experian.com/blogs/ask-experian/credit-education/improving-credit/improve-credit-score/ — 1,044 citations (1.0%)
Discover and Experian own AI answers on credit building — with zero mention of Chime's credit builder product. At 105K citations per month, this is nearly as large as Early Paycheck and completely uncontested by Chime's PR.
Priority 03 — High volume, no coverage
Overdraft Protection
75.8K
Topic citations (30d)
0
PR placements
0%
PR share
businessinsider.com/personal-finance/banking/best-banks-for-overdrafts — 1,520 citations (2.0%)
wellsfargo.com/checking/overdraft-services/ — 1,294 citations (1.7%)
Business Insider and Wells Fargo dominate AI responses on overdraft — neither references Chime's no-fee model. A placement in either would put Chime directly in front of users actively asking about overdraft solutions.
Priority 04 — Brand alignment
Second Chance Banking
53.7K
Topic citations (30d)
0
PR placements
0%
PR share
pnc.com/en/personal-banking/banking/checking/simple-checking.html — 682 citations (1.3%)
bankrate.com/banking/what-to-do-if-you-cant-open-a-bank-account/ — 564 citations (1.1%)
PNC and Bankrate lead AI answers for users who can't open a traditional account — exactly the audience Chime is built for. No-credit-check banking is a core Chime differentiator with no PR presence here.
Placements not driving citations — 11 URLs
Grouped by reason · all 30-day data
These 11 URLs are on the PR list but aren't showing up when AI tools answer banking questions. They're grouped below by the reason why — which also tells you what to do about each one.
Reason 01
Wrong content format
AI cites /best/ comparison pages — not editorials, award lists, or brand spotlights.
Action
Remove from PR list. Don't renew.
nerdwallet.com/banking/learn/neobanks-fintechs-to-watch
Editorial roundup — not a format AI cites
nerdwallet.com/l/awards-banking-2026
Awards page — AI never cites promotional formats
nerdwallet.com/banking/learn/big-bank-alternatives
/learn/ article — AI cites /best/ comparison pages, not this
Reason 02
Wrong topic for Chime
Topics where Chime has no tracked presence or isn't a natural fit for what people are asking.
Action
Remove from PR list. Redirect budget to tracked topics.
cnbc.com/select/chime-app-free-tax-filing/
26K topic citations but IRS.gov dominates — CNBC not appearing in AI answers
cnbc.com/select/what-is-a-tax-refund-advance/
Tax refund content — not a Chime visibility topic
nerdwallet.com/banking/learn/peer-to-peer-p2p-money-transfers
Untracked topic — Wise dominates P2P
nerdwallet.com/finance/learn/cash-back-apps
Small topic (7,851 citations) and /learn/ format — not breaking into top cited pages
Reason 03
Too broad or audience mismatch
Too general to rank in AI answers, or targeting audiences outside Chime's core (teens, students).
Action
Replace with targeted /best/ articles in fee-free banking, checking, or debit cards.
nerdwallet.com/banking/best/banks-and-credit-unions
Too broad — AI favors specific category pages over general directories
nerdwallet.com/banking/best/bank-accounts-for-college-students
Untracked topic — limited citation value for Chime
nerdwallet.com/banking/best/teen-checking-accounts
Untracked topic — audience doesn't match Chime's AEO profile
nerdwallet.com/banking/best/banks-and-credit-unions-for-mobile-banking
Covered under Online Banking — not breaking into top citations
Recommendations
2 actions
Keep Going
The 6 active NerdWallet placements are working — don't let them lapse. Three URLs alone (free-checking, online-checking, checking-accounts) are responsible for 79% of all AI citations Chime earns through PR. When AI tools answer questions about banking, these pages are why Chime shows up. Renew and maintain these relationships.
Stop Spending
11 URLs on the current list are generating zero AI citations — consider cutting them. This includes both CNBC articles, the NerdWallet awards page, the neobanks editorial, and several category pages that are too broad or off-topic. AI doesn't pull from awards pages or editorial roundups. That PR budget would go further on a single well-placed "best of" article in a high-traffic topic.
